exemplar.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, clear focus on the prescribed title with precise definitions of key terms that frame the discussion effectively.
Arguments are coherent and supported by well-chosen examples (e.g., electron microscopy in biology; ethical protocols in psychology).
Awareness of counterclaims and alternative perspectives (e.g., ethical constraints vs. research freedom; CRISPR off-target effects) shows evaluative engagement.
Integration of real-world applications (virus detection, He Jiankui case) demonstrates relevance and depth of research.
Comprehensive bibliography indicates thorough engagement with interdisciplinary literature.
weaknesses: Analysis of some examples (CRISPR limitations; electron microscopy biases) remains descriptive rather than fully evaluative, limiting analytical depth.
Introduction lacks a clear roadmap and justification for AOK selection, affecting overall coherence.
Overreliance on secondary sources (e.g., Wikipedia) and inconsistent citation formatting weaken academic rigor.
Conclusion restates arguments without synthesizing broader implications or providing a final evaluative judgment.
Discussion occasionally drifts into background description instead of sustaining critical evaluation of implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay clearly satisfies the descriptors for band 7â8 by maintaining a sustained focus on the title, linking arguments effectively to two areas of knowledge, and supporting claims with specific examples and counterclaims. However, analytical depth is uneven: some examples are not fully explored for their epistemological implications, and the conclusion fails to synthesize the overall impact of âever-improving toolsâ on knowledge. These shortcomings prevent it from reaching band 9â10. Allocating a score of 7 appropriately reflects the essayâs clear coherence and use of examples while accounting for its uneven evaluation and structural weaknesses.

annotations.comment: The discussion of off-target effects demonstrates awareness of how methodological limitations can undermine the reliability of CRISPR-derived knowledge.
The extensive Works Cited list reflects thorough research across disciplines, indicating strong engagement with both scientific and ethical literature.
The counterclaim using the Stanford Prison Experiment effectively illustrates how stricter ethical constraints can restrict certain types of knowledge production.
The Natural Science section opens with a general discipline definition; focus instead on how specific technological tools shape biological knowledge to maintain relevance to the title.
After describing electron microscopyâs capabilities, the student should analyze potential biases introduced by sample preparation and imaging conditions.
Reliance on Wikipedia for key historical details weakens academic rigor; the student should use primary or peer-reviewed sources for discussions of major experiments.
The electron microscopy example is well-chosen and vividly demonstrates how advances in resolution and magnification can transform our understanding of cellular structures.
Listing the five current ethical principles demonstrates systematic understanding of evolving methodological tools in psychological research.
The thesis statement is too broad and only generically links tools to knowledge; it would benefit from early reference to the chosen AOKs to sharpen focus.
Clear definitions of âever-improving toolsâ and âimproved knowledgeâ establish a solid conceptual framework; these definitions guide subsequent examples and analysis.
Ethical procedure description would benefit from a concrete example linking a specific principle directly to a study outcome; include such an illustration for clarity.
The student reproduces the prescribed title exactly, demonstrating a clear understanding of the question to be addressed and anchoring the discussion firmly in the TOK context.
Rationale for selecting Biology and Psychology as AOKs is underdeveloped; the student should explain explicitly why these areas most effectively illustrate tool-knowledge dynamics.
The parenthetical â(Imm)â appears incomplete and unexamined; ensure full citation details are provided and critically evaluate Jacob Immâs perspective on ethics.
The introduction lacks a clear roadmap of the essayâs structure; a brief outline of arguments and counterclaims would improve coherence and guide the reader.
Potential measurement biases introduced by advanced biological tools (e.g., sample artefacts) are not discussed; including this nuance would enrich the analysis.
The citation â(McLeod)â is inserted without integration into the argument; the student should articulate how McLeodâs discussion specifically supports the claim about ethical procedures.
Inclusion of CRISPRâs ethical and safety concerns adds valuable depth by acknowledging non-technical constraints on the production of genetic knowledge.
The link between improved ethical protocols and enhanced data quality is clearly explained, showing how methodological changes impact the reliability of psychological knowledge.
Discussion of participant perspectives remains largely descriptive; the student should evaluate how informed consent and debriefing procedures might introduce new biases or affect validity.
Detailed examples of virus detection (e.g., norovirus, Ebola) effectively demonstrate how improved tools enable new knowledge with direct real-world applications.
The assertion that CRISPR âdoesnât lead to improved knowledgeâ is abrupt; connect this claim explicitly back to the off-target and ethical concerns discussed earlier.
The conclusion merely restates earlier points without synthesizing the overall implications for the relationship between tools and knowledge; a final evaluative judgment is needed.
The He Jiankui case is noted but lacks exploration of its broader epistemological implications for human germline editing; consider its impact on future knowledge paradigms.
Citation formats in the Works Cited are inconsistentâsome entries lack italics or proper punctuation; apply a uniform style (e.g., APA or MLA) consistently.
There is no final synthesis addressing whether ever-improving tools can mislead or distort knowledge; the essay would benefit from a balanced closing evaluation of this possibility.

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of

Arguments are

examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
